meta.description: Advanced ML system design interview questions targeting senior and staff-level engineers

steps: Problem formulation (clarify objective, define ML task type)
Metrics (offline: AUC, precision/recall, NDCG; online: CTR, engagement, revenue)
Training pipeline (infrastructure, distributed training, hyperparameter tuning)
Serving and inference (latency, throughput, batch vs real-time)
Evaluation and experimentation (A/B testing, guardrail metrics)
Monitoring and iteration (data drift, model staleness, feedback loops)

questions.title: Design a Real-Time Bidding System
questions.description: Design an ML-powered real-time bidding (RTB) system that decides how much to bid on ad impressions within a 100ms budget, optimizing for advertiser ROI and platform revenue.
key_discussion_points: Bid optimization formulation: predict click/conversion probability, compute optimal bid price
Feature engineering: user context, ad creative features, publisher signals, time-of-day patterns, historical auction data
Model architecture: deep neural networks with cross features (DCN-v2), calibration layers for accurate probability estimation
Pacing and budget management: ensuring advertiser budgets are spent evenly across the campaign period
Exploration vs exploitation: Thompson sampling or epsilon-greedy for new creatives and audience segments
Training at scale: billions of impression-level samples per day, online learning for rapid adaptation
Serving under extreme latency constraints: model distillation, feature caching, prediction pre-computation
Feedback attribution: conversion delay handling, multi-touch attribution models
Privacy-preserving bidding: differential privacy, on-device signals, cookieless targeting

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Bid optimization formulation, CTR/CVR prediction models, feature engineering, calibration importance, basic pacing algorithm, serving latency optimization
expected_depth_by_level.E6: End-to-end auction dynamics, multi-objective bid optimization, online learning pipeline, budget pacing with control theory, privacy-preserving ML, cross-channel attribution modeling
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Bid optimization formulation, CTR/CVR prediction models, feature engineering, calibration importance, basic pacing algorithm, serving latency optimization
expected_depth_by_level.L6: End-to-end auction dynamics, multi-objective bid optimization, online learning pipeline, budget pacing with control theory, privacy-preserving ML, cross-channel attribution modeling

questions.title: Design a Language Translation System
questions.description: Design an ML system for neural machine translation that supports 100+ languages, handles low-resource language pairs, and serves translations at scale with low latency.
key_discussion_points: Model architecture: encoder-decoder transformers, multilingual vs per-language-pair models
Training data: parallel corpora, back-translation for data augmentation, synthetic data generation
Low-resource languages: transfer learning from high-resource pairs, pivot languages, zero-shot translation
Tokenization: SentencePiece, BPE with shared vocabulary across languages, handling morphologically rich languages
Training at scale: distributed training across GPU clusters, mixed precision, curriculum learning
Serving infrastructure: model sharding, batching strategies, KV-cache optimization for autoregressive decoding
Quality evaluation: BLEU, COMET, human evaluation pipelines, language-specific quality assessment
Continuous improvement: human-in-the-loop feedback, active learning for translation quality, A/B testing across language pairs
Domain adaptation: fine-tuning for legal, medical, technical domains without catastrophic forgetting

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Transformer encoder-decoder architecture, training data pipeline, BPE tokenization, BLEU evaluation, basic serving with batching, multilingual vs per-pair trade-offs
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Multilingual foundation model design, low-resource transfer learning strategies, distributed training infrastructure, quality-aware serving with model routing, domain adaptation without forgetting, human evaluation at scale
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Transformer encoder-decoder architecture, training data pipeline, BPE tokenization, BLEU evaluation, basic serving with batching, multilingual vs per-pair trade-offs
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Multilingual foundation model design, low-resource transfer learning strategies, distributed training infrastructure, quality-aware serving with model routing, domain adaptation without forgetting, human evaluation at scale

questions.title: Design an Image Generation Pipeline
questions.description: Design an ML system for text-to-image generation that produces high-quality images from text prompts, with controllability, safety guardrails, and efficient serving.
key_discussion_points: Model architecture: diffusion models (U-Net denoiser, latent diffusion), text encoder (CLIP/T5) for conditioning
Training pipeline: large-scale image-text dataset curation, filtering for quality and safety, aesthetic scoring
Controllability: classifier-free guidance, ControlNet for spatial conditioning, style transfer, inpainting
Safety and content moderation: NSFW filtering, prompt blocklisting, output classifiers, watermarking for provenance
Serving infrastructure: GPU cluster management, batching denoising steps, latency vs quality trade-offs with step count
Model optimization: knowledge distillation for fewer steps, quantization, TensorRT acceleration
Evaluation: FID, CLIP score, human preference studies, red-teaming for adversarial prompts
Personalization: fine-tuning with user-provided images (DreamBooth, LoRA), efficient adapter management
Intellectual property and copyright: training data provenance, opt-out mechanisms, content attribution

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Diffusion model fundamentals (forward/reverse process), latent diffusion architecture, text conditioning with CLIP, safety classifier pipeline, basic serving with GPU batching
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Training data curation at scale, controllability mechanisms (ControlNet, guidance), model distillation for efficient serving, personalization with efficient fine-tuning (LoRA), comprehensive safety and IP framework, human evaluation pipelines
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Diffusion model fundamentals (forward/reverse process), latent diffusion architecture, text conditioning with CLIP, safety classifier pipeline, basic serving with GPU batching
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Training data curation at scale, controllability mechanisms (ControlNet, guidance), model distillation for efficient serving, personalization with efficient fine-tuning (LoRA), comprehensive safety and IP framework, human evaluation pipelines

questions.title: Design a Conversational AI System
questions.description: Design an ML system for a multi-turn conversational AI assistant that can handle open-domain chat, task completion, and knowledge-grounded responses at scale.
key_discussion_points: Model architecture: large language model (decoder-only transformer), instruction tuning, RLHF/DPO alignment
Context management: conversation history, context window optimization, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG)
Training pipeline: pre-training data curation, SFT data quality, reward model training, reinforcement learning from human feedback
Safety and alignment: constitutional AI, red-teaming, refusal training, jailbreak detection
Tool use and function calling: structured output generation, API integration, action execution
Serving infrastructure: KV-cache management, speculative decoding, continuous batching, model parallelism
Multi-turn dialog: state tracking, coreference resolution, topic switching, user personalization
Evaluation: human preference ratings, automated benchmarks, safety evaluations, task-completion metrics
Responsible AI: bias mitigation, factuality verification, hallucination reduction, transparency and explainability

expected_depth_by_level.E5: LLM architecture choices, instruction tuning pipeline, RAG for knowledge grounding, KV-cache serving optimization, safety classifier integration, basic evaluation framework
expected_depth_by_level.E6: RLHF/DPO training pipeline design, advanced serving (speculative decoding, continuous batching), tool-use architecture, comprehensive safety framework, multi-turn state management, platform-level infrastructure for serving LLMs at scale
expected_depth_by_level.L5: LLM architecture choices, instruction tuning pipeline, RAG for knowledge grounding, KV-cache serving optimization, safety classifier integration, basic evaluation framework
expected_depth_by_level.L6: RLHF/DPO training pipeline design, advanced serving (speculative decoding, continuous batching), tool-use architecture, comprehensive safety framework, multi-turn state management, platform-level infrastructure for serving LLMs at scale

questions.title: Design an Anomaly Detection Platform
questions.description: Design a general-purpose ML platform for detecting anomalies across diverse data streams including time-series metrics, log data, network traffic, and financial transactions.
key_discussion_points: Problem taxonomy: point anomalies, contextual anomalies, collective anomalies across different data modalities
Model approaches: statistical methods (Z-score, STL decomposition), isolation forests, autoencoders, transformer-based time-series models
Feature engineering: temporal patterns, seasonality, trend decomposition, cross-metric correlations
Multi-tenancy: per-metric model vs shared model with metric embeddings, automatic hyperparameter tuning per stream
Training pipeline: self-supervised learning (reconstruction loss), handling label scarcity with active learning
Real-time scoring: streaming inference with sliding windows, sub-second detection latency
Alert management: severity scoring, alert deduplication, root cause grouping, reducing alert fatigue
Evaluation: precision/recall with delayed ground truth, time-to-detect metrics, false positive rate at operator-level
Platform design: onboarding new data sources, configurable detection sensitivity, integration with incident management

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Anomaly types and model selection, autoencoder-based detection, temporal feature engineering, streaming inference architecture, alert severity scoring, evaluation methodology
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Multi-tenant platform with per-stream adaptation, self-supervised training at scale, cross-metric correlation detection, automated root cause analysis, platform API design for onboarding, organization-wide anomaly detection strategy
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Anomaly types and model selection, autoencoder-based detection, temporal feature engineering, streaming inference architecture, alert severity scoring, evaluation methodology
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Multi-tenant platform with per-stream adaptation, self-supervised training at scale, cross-metric correlation detection, automated root cause analysis, platform API design for onboarding, organization-wide anomaly detection strategy

questions.title: Design a Content Moderation System at Scale
questions.description: Design an ML system that moderates user-generated content across text, images, video, and audio at the scale of billions of posts per day, balancing safety with free expression.
key_discussion_points: Multi-modal classification: separate and fused models for text, image, video, and audio content
Policy taxonomy: hate speech, violence, nudity, misinformation, spam, harassment, self-harm categories with severity levels
Model architecture: transformer-based text models, vision transformers for images, temporal models for video, multi-modal fusion with cross-attention
Training data: human labeling pipelines, inter-annotator agreement, active learning for hard cases, handling policy changes
Precision-recall trade-offs: per-category thresholds, severity-based escalation, appeal handling
Adversarial robustness: text obfuscation, steganography in images, adversarial training and data augmentation
Human review optimization: routing to specialized reviewers, review queue prioritization, reviewer well-being considerations
Serving at scale: tiered moderation (lightweight pre-filter, full model, human review), latency budgets for upload-time vs feed-time moderation
Evaluation: policy-specific metrics, cross-cultural fairness audits, false positive impact on marginalized communities

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Multi-modal classification architecture, policy taxonomy mapping to ML tasks, precision/recall threshold management, human review queue integration, adversarial content handling
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Multi-modal fusion research, policy-as-code framework for rapid category updates, cross-cultural fairness evaluation, adversarial robustness pipeline, human review optimization with active learning, organization-wide moderation platform design
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Multi-modal classification architecture, policy taxonomy mapping to ML tasks, precision/recall threshold management, human review queue integration, adversarial content handling
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Multi-modal fusion research, policy-as-code framework for rapid category updates, cross-cultural fairness evaluation, adversarial robustness pipeline, human review optimization with active learning, organization-wide moderation platform design

questions.title: Design a Personalized Email Marketing ML System
questions.description: Design an ML system that personalizes email marketing campaigns, optimizing what content to send, to whom, when, and how often, maximizing conversions while minimizing unsubscribes.
key_discussion_points: User segmentation: behavioral clustering, RFM analysis, propensity modeling for purchase likelihood
Send-time optimization: per-user optimal delivery time prediction based on open/click history
Content personalization: product recommendation within email, dynamic subject line generation, template selection
Volume optimization: reinforcement learning for per-user frequency capping, fatigue modeling
Multi-touch attribution: credit assignment across email, push, in-app channels using causal inference
Training pipeline: large-scale user behavior data, handling selection bias (only see outcomes for sent emails), inverse propensity weighting
Model architecture: multi-task learning for open/click/convert/unsubscribe prediction, calibrated probabilities
Uplift modeling: estimating incremental impact of sending vs not sending (causal ML)
Evaluation: incremental revenue per email, long-term user LTV impact, holdout group experimentation

expected_depth_by_level.E5: User segmentation and propensity scoring, send-time optimization, content personalization models, multi-task prediction for engagement outcomes, basic A/B testing framework
expected_depth_by_level.E6: Uplift modeling for causal impact estimation, RL-based frequency optimization, selection bias correction, multi-touch attribution with causal inference, long-term LTV optimization, platform design for campaign orchestration at scale
expected_depth_by_level.L5: User segmentation and propensity scoring, send-time optimization, content personalization models, multi-task prediction for engagement outcomes, basic A/B testing framework
expected_depth_by_level.L6: Uplift modeling for causal impact estimation, RL-based frequency optimization, selection bias correction, multi-touch attribution with causal inference, long-term LTV optimization, platform design for campaign orchestration at scale

questions.title: Design a Self-Driving Car Perception System
questions.description: Design the ML perception stack for an autonomous vehicle that detects and tracks objects, understands road scenes, and predicts the behavior of other road users in real time.
key_discussion_points: Sensor fusion: camera, LiDAR, radar data alignment and fusion strategies (early vs late fusion)
3D object detection: point cloud processing (PointPillars, CenterPoint), camera-based 3D detection (BEV projection)
Multi-object tracking: data association, Kalman filtering, track lifecycle management
Semantic scene understanding: lane detection, drivable area segmentation, traffic sign/light recognition
Behavior prediction: trajectory forecasting for vehicles, pedestrians, cyclists using graph neural networks and transformers
Training data: large-scale driving datasets, simulation-generated data, auto-labeling with foundation models
Model architecture: bird's-eye-view (BEV) backbone for unified multi-sensor representation
Real-time inference: model optimization for on-vehicle compute (TensorRT, INT8 quantization), strict latency budgets (<50ms)
Safety: redundancy across sensor modalities, uncertainty estimation, out-of-distribution detection, formal verification

expected_depth_by_level.E5: Camera and LiDAR processing pipelines, 3D object detection architectures, multi-object tracking, BEV representation, model optimization for on-vehicle inference, core evaluation metrics (AP, MOTA)
expected_depth_by_level.E6: End-to-end sensor fusion architecture, trajectory prediction with interaction modeling, simulation-to-real domain adaptation, uncertainty-aware perception, auto-labeling pipeline design, safety validation framework, perception platform architecture for fleet-wide model deployment
expected_depth_by_level.L5: Camera and LiDAR processing pipelines, 3D object detection architectures, multi-object tracking, BEV representation, model optimization for on-vehicle inference, core evaluation metrics (AP, MOTA)
expected_depth_by_level.L6: End-to-end sensor fusion architecture, trajectory prediction with interaction modeling, simulation-to-real domain adaptation, uncertainty-aware perception, auto-labeling pipeline design, safety validation framework, perception platform architecture for fleet-wide model deployment
